meta.title: Learn Claude Code
meta.description: Build a nano Claude Code-like agent from 0 to 1, one mechanism at a time

home.hero_subtitle: Build a nano Claude Code-like agent from 0 to 1, one mechanism at a time
home.core_pattern_desc: Every AI coding agent shares the same loop: call the model, execute tools, feed results back. Production systems add policy, permissions, and lifecycle layers on top.
home.learning_path_desc: 12 progressive sessions, from a simple loop to isolated autonomous execution
home.layers_desc: Five orthogonal concerns that compose into a complete agent
home.message_flow_desc: Watch the messages array grow as the agent loop executes

sim.begin_hint: Press Play or Step to begin

timeline.title: Learning Path
timeline.subtitle: s01 to s12: Progressive Agent Design

layers.title: Architectural Layers
layers.subtitle: Five orthogonal concerns that compose into a complete agent
layers.tools: What the agent CAN do. The foundation: tools give the model capabilities to interact with the world.
layers.planning: How work is organized. From simple todo lists to dependency-aware task boards shared across agents.
layers.memory: Keeping context within limits. Compression strategies that let agents work infinitely without losing coherence.
layers.concurrency: Non-blocking execution. Background threads and notification buses for parallel work.
layers.collaboration: Multi-agent coordination. Teams, messaging, and autonomous teammates that think for themselves.

compare.title: Compare Versions
compare.subtitle: See what changed between any two versions
compare.empty_hint: Select two versions above to compare them.

viz_steps.s01_0_desc: Every agent is a while loop that keeps calling the model until it says 'stop'.
viz_steps.s01_1_desc: The loop starts when the user sends a message.
viz_steps.s01_2_desc: Send all messages to the LLM. It sees everything and decides what to do.
viz_steps.s01_3_desc: The model wants to use a tool. The loop continues.
viz_steps.s01_4_desc: Run the tool, append the result to messages[]. Feed it back.
viz_steps.s01_5_desc: Same code path, second iteration. The model decides to edit a file.
viz_steps.s01_6_desc: The model is done. Loop exits. That's the entire agent.
viz_steps.s02_0_desc: A dictionary maps tool names to handler functions. The loop code never changes.
viz_steps.s02_2_desc: Same pattern, different handler. Validate input, execute, return result.
viz_steps.s02_3_desc: Every tool returns a tool_result that goes back into messages[].
viz_steps.s02_4_desc: Adding a new tool = adding one entry to the dispatch map.
viz_steps.s02_5_desc: The while loop stays the same. You only grow the dispatch map. That's it.
viz_steps.s03_0_desc: TodoWrite gives the model a visible plan. All tasks start as pending.
viz_steps.s03_1_desc: The model does work but doesn't touch its todos. The nag counter increments.
viz_steps.s03_2_desc: Two rounds without progress. Pressure builds.
viz_steps.s03_3_desc: Threshold reached! System message injected: 'You have pending tasks. Pick one up now!'
viz_steps.s03_4_desc: The model completes the task. Timer stays at 0 -- working on todos resets the counter.
viz_steps.s03_5_desc: Once the model learns the pattern, it picks up tasks voluntarily.
viz_steps.s03_6_desc: Visible plan + nag pressure = reliable task completion.
viz_steps.s04_0_desc: The parent agent has accumulated messages from the conversation.
viz_steps.s04_1_desc: Task tool creates a child with fresh messages[]. Only the task description is passed.
viz_steps.s04_2_desc: The child has its own context. It doesn't see the parent's history.
viz_steps.s04_3_desc: The child's full conversation compresses into one summary.
viz_steps.s04_4_desc: Only the summary returns. The child's full context is discarded.
viz_steps.s04_5_desc: The parent gets a clean summary without context bloat. This is fresh-context isolation via messages[].
viz_steps.s05_0_desc: All skills are summarized in the system prompt. Compact, always present.
viz_steps.s05_1_desc: The model recognizes a skill invocation and triggers the Skill tool.
viz_steps.s05_2_desc: The full skill instructions are injected as a tool_result, not into the system prompt.
viz_steps.s05_3_desc: The detailed instructions appear as if a tool returned them. The model follows them precisely.
viz_steps.s05_4_desc: Multiple skills can be loaded. Only summaries are permanent; full content comes and goes.
viz_steps.s05_5_desc: Layer 1: always present, tiny. Layer 2: loaded on demand, detailed. Elegant separation.
viz_steps.s06_0_desc: The context window holds the conversation. Each API call adds more messages.
viz_steps.s06_1_desc: As the agent works, messages accumulate. The context window fills up.
viz_steps.s06_2_desc: Old tool_results are the biggest consumers. Micro-compact targets these first.
viz_steps.s06_3_desc: Replace old tool_results with short summaries. Automatic, transparent to the model.
viz_steps.s06_4_desc: Work continues. Context grows again toward the threshold...
viz_steps.s06_5_desc: Entire conversation summarized into a compact block. Triggered at token threshold.
viz_steps.s06_6_desc: User-triggered, most aggressive. Three layers of strategic forgetting enable infinite sessions.
viz_steps.s07_0_desc: Tasks are stored in JSON files on disk. They survive context compaction -- unlike in-memory state.
viz_steps.s07_1_desc: Tasks without dependencies can start immediately. T1 has no blockers.
viz_steps.s07_2_desc: Completing T1 unblocks its dependents: T2 and T3.
viz_steps.s07_3_desc: T2 and T3 have no dependency on each other. Both can run simultaneously.
viz_steps.s07_4_desc: T4 depends on BOTH T2 and T3. It waits for all blockers to complete.
viz_steps.s07_5_desc: All blockers resolved. T4 can now proceed.
viz_steps.s07_6_desc: The entire dependency graph is resolved. File-based persistence means this works across context compressions.
viz_steps.s08_0_desc: The agent has a main thread and can spawn daemon background threads for parallel work.
viz_steps.s08_1_desc: The main agent loop runs as usual, processing user requests.
viz_steps.s08_2_desc: Background tasks run as daemon threads. The main loop doesn't wait for them.
viz_steps.s08_3_desc: Multiple background tasks can run concurrently.
viz_steps.s08_4_desc: Background task finishes. Its result goes to the notification queue.
viz_steps.s08_5_desc: Results accumulate in the queue, invisible to the model during this turn.
viz_steps.s08_6_desc: Just before the next LLM call, all queued notifications are injected as tool_results. Non-blocking, async.
viz_steps.s09_0_desc: Teams use a leader-worker pattern. Each teammate has a file-based mailbox inbox.
viz_steps.s09_1_desc: Communication is async: write a message to the recipient's .jsonl inbox file.
viz_steps.s09_2_desc: Teammates poll their inbox before each LLM call. New messages become context.
viz_steps.s09_3_desc: Each teammate runs its own agent loop independently.
viz_steps.s09_4_desc: Results flow through the same mailbox mechanism. All communication is via files.
viz_steps.s09_5_desc: The mailbox pattern supports any communication topology: linear, broadcast, round-robin.
viz_steps.s09_6_desc: No shared memory, no locks. All coordination through append-only files. Simple, robust, debuggable.
viz_steps.s10_0_desc: Protocols define structured message exchanges with correlated request IDs.
viz_steps.s10_1_desc: The leader initiates shutdown. The request_id links the request to its response.
viz_steps.s10_2_desc: The teammate can accept or reject. It's not a forced kill -- it's a polite request.
viz_steps.s10_3_desc: Same request_id in the response. Teammate exits cleanly.
viz_steps.s10_4_desc: Teammates in plan_mode must get approval before implementing changes.
viz_steps.s10_5_desc: The teammate designs a plan and sends it to the leader for review.
viz_steps.s10_6_desc: Leader reviews and approves or rejects with feedback. Same request-response pattern.
viz_steps.s11_0_desc: Autonomous agents need no coordinator. They govern themselves with an idle-poll-claim-work cycle.
viz_steps.s11_1_desc: Each idle agent counts rounds. A timeout triggers self-directed task polling.
viz_steps.s11_2_desc: Timeout! The agent reads the task board looking for unclaimed work.
viz_steps.s11_3_desc: The agent writes its name to the task record. Atomic, no conflicts.
viz_steps.s11_4_desc: The agent works on the claimed task using its own agent loop.
viz_steps.s11_5_desc: Multiple agents poll and claim independently. No central coordinator needed.
viz_steps.s11_6_desc: Task done. Agent returns to idle. The cycle repeats.
viz_steps.s11_7_desc: Three agents, zero coordination overhead. Polling + timeout = emergent organization.
viz_steps.s12_0_desc: Two tasks are active, but both edits would hit one directory and collide.
viz_steps.s12_1_desc: Create a worktree lane and associate it with task 1 for clear ownership.
viz_steps.s12_2_desc: Lane creation and task association can be separate. Here task 2 binds after lane creation.
viz_steps.s12_3_desc: Each command routes by selected lane directory, not by the shared root.
viz_steps.s12_4_desc: Closeout can mix decisions: keep ui-login active for follow-up, remove auth-refactor and complete task 1.
viz_steps.s12_5_desc: The board tracks shared truth, worktree lanes isolate execution, and events provide auditable side-channel traces.

showcase.title: Showcase
showcase.subtitle: Interactive step-by-step PPT demonstrations for all 12 tasks. Perfect for recording tutorials.
showcase.select_task: Select a task to start your showcase journey

version_meta.s01_keyInsight: The minimal agent kernel is a while loop + one tool
version_meta.s02_keyInsight: The loop stays the same; new tools register into the dispatch map
version_meta.s03_keyInsight: An agent without a plan drifts; list the steps first, then execute
version_meta.s04_keyInsight: Subagents use independent messages[], keeping the main conversation clean
version_meta.s05_keyInsight: Inject knowledge via tool_result when needed, not upfront in the system prompt
version_meta.s06_keyInsight: Context will fill up; three-layer compression strategy enables infinite sessions
version_meta.s07_keyInsight: A file-based task graph with ordering, parallelism, and dependencies -- the coordination backbone for multi-agent work
version_meta.s07_coreAddition: TaskManager with file-based state + dependency graph
version_meta.s08_keyInsight: Run slow operations in the background; the agent keeps thinking ahead
version_meta.s09_keyInsight: When one agent can't finish, delegate to persistent teammates via async mailboxes
version_meta.s10_keyInsight: One request-response pattern drives all team negotiation
version_meta.s11_keyInsight: Teammates scan the board and claim tasks themselves; no need for the lead to assign each one
version_meta.s12_keyInsight: Each works in its own directory; tasks manage goals, worktrees manage directories, bound by ID
version_meta.s12_coreAddition: Composable worktree lifecycle + event stream over a shared task board
